claims.evidence_text: Early planted wheat may also create a green bridge between wheat crops that serve as alternate hosts for important winter wheat pests. For example, winter wheat seedlings can be infected by barley yellow dwarf virus or wheat streak mosaic virus, which can lead to a significant reduction in yields.
claims.claim_summary: Early planting creates a 'green bridge' increasing risk of barley yellow dwarf virus and wheat streak mosaic virus infection of winter wheat seedlings.

claims.evidence_text: Seeding winter wheat into broadleaf crop stubble is recommended to reduce insect, disease and weed problems. Even though seeding into wheat stubble is common, disease risks are associated with this practice.
claims.claim_summary: Seeding winter wheat into broadleaf crop stubble is recommended to reduce insect, disease and weed problems.

claims.evidence_text: if a certain disease is expected to be a problem in a specific region in a particular year, using a winter wheat variety with resistance traits to that particular disease can minimize yield losses.
claims.claim_summary: Selecting a winter wheat variety with resistance traits to a regionally important disease minimizes yield losses.

claims.evidence_text: Plants at the reproductive stages (late boot to heading) are most sensitive to freeze injury (Table 4.1). At this stage, temperatures slightly below freezing can severely injure wheat, causing considerable yield reduction.
claims.claim_summary: Winter wheat is most sensitive to spring freeze injury at the reproductive stages (late boot to heading).

claims.evidence_text: Tillering 12° F Leaf chlorosis; burning leaf tips; silage odor Slight to moderate
claims.claim_summary: Spring freeze injury threshold at tillering: 12°F (2 hr exposure) causes leaf chlorosis/burning, slight to moderate yield impact.
claims.table_label: Table 4.1. Temperatures, symptoms, and yield impacts from exposure to 2 hours of cool temperature at selected growth stages

claims.evidence_text: Jointing 24° F Death of growing point; leaf yellowing or burning; lesions, odor Moderate to severe
claims.claim_summary: Spring freeze injury threshold at jointing: 24°F (2 hr exposure) causes death of growing point, moderate to severe yield impact.
claims.table_label: Table 4.1. Temperatures, symptoms, and yield impacts from exposure to 2 hours of cool temperature at selected growth stages

claims.evidence_text: Boot 28° F Floret sterility; spike trapped in the boot; damage to lower stem; leaf discoloration, odor Moderate to severe
claims.claim_summary: Spring freeze injury threshold at boot: 28°F (2 hr exposure) causes floret sterility, moderate to severe yield impact.
claims.table_label: Table 4.1. Temperatures, symptoms, and yield impacts from exposure to 2 hours of cool temperature at selected growth stages

claims.evidence_text: Heading 30° F Floret sterility; white awns or spikes; damage to lower stem; leaf discoloration Severe
claims.claim_summary: Spring freeze injury threshold at heading: 30°F (2 hr exposure) causes floret sterility, severe yield impact.
claims.table_label: Table 4.1. Temperatures, symptoms, and yield impacts from exposure to 2 hours of cool temperature at selected growth stages

claims.evidence_text: Flowering 30° F Floret sterility; white awns or spikes; damage to lower stem; leaf discoloration Severe
claims.claim_summary: Spring freeze injury threshold at flowering (anthesis): 30°F (2 hr exposure) causes floret sterility, severe yield impact.
claims.table_label: Table 4.1. Temperatures, symptoms, and yield impacts from exposure to 2 hours of cool temperature at selected growth stages
